title: Bounded Node Upload Admission and Quarantine · Contract Defense
summary: A production board compares near-miss TypeScript and AWS decisions for bounded node upload admission and quarantine and accepts only runtime validation, bounded ownership, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit controlled AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for Node upload-admission workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat filenames as display metadata, normalize safely, reject controls and path confusion, and generate storage keys independently.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine bounded signature inspection, declared media type, extension, parser or scanner evidence, and allow-list policy without executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Upload Byte Time Count Budget · changed case · Quiz Upload Byte Time…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 5395 during a multi-tenant authorization audit. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to validate upload byte and concurrency budgets before a quarantined state transition; do not treat TypeScript types, a resolved promise, CORS, ETag, or an emulator as stronger proof than it is; then deliver a duplicate or reordered event and prevent duplicate stakeholder effect. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, enforce per-file, request, tenant, concurrent, time, and aggregate byte budgets before and during transfer.
